title: U.S. and Iran reach peace deal to end the Mideast war, with agreement set to be signed Friday
description: The deal follows weeks of mixed messaging from both Washington and Tehran on the trajectory of the conflict.

title: World leaders welcome U.S.-Iran deal as Europe signals sanctions relief, urges Hormuz reopening
description: The agreement came after more than three months of stop-start negotiations and bouts of fighting since late February, roiling global energy and commodities markets.

title: SpaceX: To the moon for investors or a bumpy ride? Here's what experts say
description: If the first day's share price is anything to go by, investors are still very optimistic on the stock.

title: SoftBank surges more than 12% as Iran-U.S. peace deal sends Asia stocks soaring
description: Asian tech stocks surged Monday on news that Iran and the U.S. have reached a peace deal

title: Markets cheer U.S.-Iran agreement, but some investors caution deal is yet to be signed
description: Asian stocks rallied Monday while oil prices tumbled after the U.S. and Iran agreed to a peace deal aimed at ending nearly four months of conflict.

title: Stock futures jump, oil prices fall as Trump says U.S. has reached peace deal with Iran
description: Oil prices fell and U.S. stock-index futures jumped Sunday after President Donald Trump said the U.S. has agreed to a peace deal with Iran, apparently ending months of hostilities that shut the Strait of Hormuz and sent the global economy into an oil shock.

title: The spectacle of SpaceX trumps DCF models
description: The public focused on the mission, not the multiple

title: Sweden demands other EU states tackle Russian oil tankers in their waters
description: Also in this newsletter: Kallas fronts up amid EEAS reform talk

title: The inescapable return of an old financial framework
description: Understanding both the ‘sources and uses’ of investible capital will become paramount as competition for funds rises

title: Lessons from Ireland’s Revolut revolution
description: The fintech’s model is proving more suitable for international scaling than the often outdated systems of legacy rivals

title: Asian stocks soar with ‘risk on’ rally after US-Iran peace deal
description: Investor euphoria following Friday’s record SpaceX IPO helps propel Japanese and Korean markets

title: eToro exploring acquisitions amid push into traditional banking services
description: Digital trading platform could apply for banking licence

title: US, Iran agree ceasefire deal as Trump heads to G7 summit
description: The US-Iran war will be top of mind at the annual meeting of wealthy nations

title: Oil falls on US-Iran deal but Hormuz backlog may last weeks
description: Prices slip even as analysts warn flows may be slow to recover and remain vulnerable to renewed disruption

title: Singapore to launch gold clearing system in bid to become precious metals hub
description: City-state seeks to shake up trade dominated by London, New York and Switzerland

title: Business Education: Financial Training
description: The FT’s 2026 Masters in Finance Ranking. Plus: tough jobs market drives applications; ‘near-peer’ mentoring; skills for the AI era; calls for more private markets teaching; why soft skills matter in hard times; studying full- or part-time

title: Students push business schools to beef up private markets teaching
description: Executives say their industry has diversified faster than the curriculum

title: China slams Indonesia investment climate over nickel curbs
description: Regulatory changes could threaten $50bn of investment, embassy warns

title: Eyre: US Iran Deal Will Not Lead to a new Middle East
description: US and Iran have reached an interim agreement to reopen the Strait of Hormuz paving the way for talks on Tehran's nuclear program and an end to a war that has engulfed the Middle East. Alan Eyre, Former Senior US Diplomat and Distinguished Diplomatic Fellow at Middle East Institute spoke to Bloomberg’s Abeer Abu Omar on Horizons Middle East and Africa on what this deal will bring to the region. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: What the US-Iran Peace Deal Means for the Strait of Hormuz
description: No region of the world produces more oil and gas than the countries straddling the Persian Gulf. Most of this energy can only be exported aboard tankers that cross the Strait of Hormuz — a waterway that’s effectively been closed for more than three months since the US and Israel started a war against Iran in late February.

title: How the IMF's Kristalina Georgieva Hires Top Talent
description: IMF Managing Director Kristalina Georgieva tells Francine Lacqua the interview question she asks every candidate, reflects on a career setback that ultimately changed her life, and shares what keeps her up at night as she prepares for the next global shock. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: K-Content Powerhouse JoongAng Group Seeks Court Rehabilitation
description: The holding company of South Korea’s JoongAng Group, a sprawling media conglomerate and K-Content powerhouse behind hits like Netflix smash Culinary Class Wars, filed for court rehabilitation, signaling a deepening liquidity crunch.

title: US and Iran Reach Deal to Reopen Hormuz
description: The US and Iran said they reached an interim agreement to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, halting a war that killed thousands of people and setting the stage for 60 days of negotiations on the fate of Tehran’s nuclear program. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Treasuries Rally as Traders Trim Fed Hike Bets After Iran Deal
description: Treasuries advanced across the curve as investors dialed back expectations for Federal Reserve interest-rate hikes following news of a deal to halt the Iran war.

title: Philippines’ Marcos Calls for Special Congress Session Amid Deadlock
description: Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. called Congress to a special session on June 17 to pass priority legislative measures, hoping lawmakers will set aside political distractions.

title: Hormuz Halt Forces Middle East Trade Into Huge Rewiring
description: In the wake of the 12-day US-Israel and Iran war a year ago, Siemens Energy AG sent a person driving from Saudi Arabia’s Red Sea port of Jeddah across the Arabian peninsula to the industrial hub of Dammam to study an alternative route in case the crucial Strait of Hormuz was shut down.

title: Cement, Textile Stocks Biggest Winners From Pakistan Budget
description: The government’s move to lower taxes on certain sectors including cement and textiles is likely to support related shares as Pakistan looks to balance growth and compliance with its promises to the International Monetary Fund.

title: LBMA Says Gold's Status as a Safe Haven Still Holds
description: London Bullion Market Association CEO Ruth Crowell thinks the narrative of gold's status as a safe haven still holds. She adds that it's important to measure safe haven status in years rather than weeks or months. She speaks with Paul Allen on "Insight with Haslinda Amin." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Lubrizol Aims to Double India Rev. Over Next 5 Years
description: Lubrizol Corp. President & CEO Rebecca Liebert expects to double its revenues in India over the next five years, and says the company has put a lot of investment in this market to enable that growth. She speaks with Paul Allen on "Insight with Haslinda Amin." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Failed SpaceX IPO Allocation Prompts Korea to Widen Mirae Review
description: South Korea’s financial watchdog has expanded an inspection of Mirae Asset Securities Co. to examine the brokerage’s failure to secure an allocation of SpaceX shares in the company’s initial public offering, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Warsh Is Inheriting a Rates-Mountain Breakdown
description: We’re going from a steady descent on interest rates to a potentially wild ride.

title: CSIS' Cahill on US-Iran Deal Impact on Energy
description: Ben Cahill, non-resident senior associate for the Energy Security and Climate Change Program at CSIS, says even though the US and Iran were able to come to an agreement to halt the ongoing war and reopen the strait of Hormuz, energy markets "are not quite out of the woods yet", and there is still room for prices to run up even after the MOU is signed. He speaks with Shery Ahn and Haidi Stroud Watts on "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade". (Source: Bloomberg)

title: 'Proud Moment for Pakistan': Finance Minister on US-Iran Agreement
description: Pakistani Finance Minister Muhammad Aurangzeb speaks with Bloomberg Television about the "critical role" his country has played in facilitating an interim agreement between the US and Iran. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Pakistan Says It's in 'Very Good Place on Fiscal Side'
description: Pakistan Finance Minister Muhammad Aurangzeb believes the country is in a 'very good place on the fiscal side'. He speaks with Paul Allen on "Insight with Haslinda Amin." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Stocks Rally, Oil Falls on Long-Awaited Iran Peace Deal | The Asia Trade 6/15/2026
description: "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade" brings you everything you need to know to get ahead as the trading day begins in Asia. Bloomberg TV is live from Tokyo and Sydney with Shery Ahn and Haidi Stroud-Watts, getting insight and analysis from newsmakers and industry leaders on the biggest stories shaping global markets. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Korea, US Agree Cooperation on Won Weakness, Seoul Official Says
description: South Korea and the US agreed to maintain close cooperation in addressing the won’s weakness, according to Korea’s top foreign-exchange official.

title: US and Iran Reach Deal to Halt the War, Reopen Hormuz
description: The US and Iran reached an interim agreement to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, halting a war that killed thousands of people and setting the stage for negotiations on the fate of Iran's nuclear program. Bloomberg's Abeer Abu Omar has the latest. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US-Iran Peace Deal May Keep Rally in India Stocks, Rupee Going
description: The rupee has emerged as Asia’s best performer over the past month.

title: SVB Energy's Vakhshouri on the Outlook for Oil Prices
description: Sara Vakhshouri, founder of SVB Energy International, says the oil market has adapted to the Strait of Hormuz disruption and prices could fall to pre-war levels if the waterway "truly" reopens. She speaks with David Ingles and Yvonne Man on Bloomberg's The China Show. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Zhipu Shares Surge 48% After JPMorgan Picks Company as AI Winner
description: Shares of Chinese AI model maker Zhipu surged after JPMorgan Chase & Co. raised the stock’s price target and picked it as a winner against close rival MiniMax.

title: Shipowners Seek Clarity on Hormuz Deal as 600 Vessels Eye Exit
description: A deal that could reopen the Strait of Hormuz within days has been met with caution among shipowners and traders, with many saying they would wait for more details in order to assess whether safe transits are possible after months of false starts.

title: Copper Pops Higher as US-Iran Agreement Puts Bulls in Charge
description: Copper rose as an interim peace agreement between the US and Iran eased fears over global economic growth and stoked optimism for metals demand.

title: Singapore to Launch Gold Clearing With JPMorgan, Other Banks
description: Singapore plans to launch a gold-clearing system this year, with banks including JPMorgan Chase & Co. and Deutsche Bank AG set to participate in the city-state’s push to become a hub in the global bullion market.

title: Trump heralds Iran deal but questions - and risks - remain
description: The announcement of an Iran deal was a welcome birthday gift for the president – but its success or failure may hinge on the details.

title: US-Iran deal eases uncertainty over the war - but there is much still to play out
description: Pakistan says the deal includes Lebanon, but recent ceasefires there have failed to take hold.

title: Oil prices slide after US-Iran deal announced
description: Under the agreement, the key Strait of Hormuz waterway will be reopened, US President Donald Trump said.

title: Russian strikes kill nine in Ukraine and damage historic cathedral, officials say
description: A Ukrainian drone attack in the Russian city of Tula, south of Moscow, killed three people and wounded three others.

title: Man apologises for making racist gesture at Korean in World Cup match
description: He was caught pulling the corners of his eyes at a South Korean influencer during the match in Mexico.

title: Trump hosts UFC cage fight on the White House lawn
description: Thousands were expected to attend the mixed martial arts fight, which coincides with Trump's 80th birthday.

title: Delhi's temperature showed 43.5C. Why did it feel hotter?
description: We spent a day out in the city with a thermal camera, recording surface temperatures indoors and outdoors.

title: Demand at baby bank 'has never been higher'
description: The founder says rising childcare costs mean many of those they help are in work but struggling.

title: World leaders welcome news that the Strait of Hormuz could soon reopen.
U.S. and Iran Reach Agreement to Reopen Strait and Begin Nuclear Talks
description: The accord halts hostilities in the war that began in February but leaves unresolved the critical issue of Iran’s nuclear program.

title: Singer Oliver Tree Is Said to Have Died in Collision of Helicopters in Brazil
description: The two helicopters crashed in Rio de Janeiro on Sunday, killing at least six people, the authorities said.

title: Trump Claims Strait Will Be ‘Permanently Toll-Free’ Under Agreement With Iran
description: In a call to The New York Times, President Trump praised Russia’s and China’s leaders and described Israel’s prime minister as “a very difficult guy.”

title: Hormuz Reopening Would Offer Relief for Asia, but Economic Scars Will Remain
description: For months, Asia has suffered a physical supply crunch that will likely drag on its economies long after the crucial waterway reopens.

title: The End of Neoliberalism
description: The virtues it extolled—cosmopolitanism and competition—led to its demise.

title: The End of the U.S.-Israel Alliance
description: A joint war against Iran might be its apex.

title: The End of Trans-Atlanticism
description: As an ideology, it’s over. But the relationship is not.

title: The End of Climate Politics
description: As the West debated a green energy future, Beijing was building it.

title: The End of the United Nations
description: To its most powerful member, it is worth more alive than dead.

title: The End of Asylum
description: States across the global north are shutting their doors permanently.

title: The End of Political Parties
description: They are hardly prepared for the new system.

title: The End of Chinese Growth
description: Beijing’s economic ambitions have been brought back to earth.

title: The End of Morality
description: We may come to miss the hypocrisy.

title: The End of the Future
description: Only Silicon Valley looks ahead now.

title: Sweden beat Tunisia 5-1 in strong start to World Cup
description: Two goals from Ayari and one each from Isak, Gyokeres and Svanberg take Sweden to the top of Group F in Monterrey.

title: Uruguay squad lands in US after flight delay ahead of World Cup opener
description: Uruguay arrived in the US less than 24 hours before the kickoff time for their game against Saudi Arabia.

title: Lebanese remain sceptical despite US-Iran ceasefire announcement
description: The US-Iran ceasefire announcement has raised hopes in Lebanon.

title: Ivory Coast beats Ecuador 1-0 at FIFA World Cup with Amad Diallo goal
description: Ecuador looked strong before Diallo broke through in the 90th minute with a clinical left-footed shot to seal the win.

title: Ecuadorian World Cup fans takeover Philly’s Rocky Steps
description: Ecuadorian football fans packed Philadelphia’s Rocky Steps to cheer on the South American nation in its opening

title: US-Iran to sign a ‘peace deal’ on Friday: What we know so far
description: Trump says a US-Iran deal will be signed this Friday, as Tehran confirms plans to end hostilities.

title: Trump allies cheer Iran ‘peace deal’ announcement as Democrats seek clarity
description: US and Iran say memorandum of understanding to be signed on Friday, but no official terms yet released.

title: Zimbabwe’s climate migrants fear eviction as crackdown intensifies
description: Many settled in Zimbabwe’s fertile Eastern Highlands after drought left their home areas unable to support farming.

title: Iran’s Ghalenoei, Taremi decry US treatment before first World Cup game
description: Striker Mehdi Taremi says US travel policies are dampening the usual joy the football tournament ignites.

title: Stock markets soar, oil falls as US, Iran confirm deal to end war
description: Asian stock markets surge as Washington and Tehran announce agreement to end hostilities and reopen Strait of Hormuz.

title: AI and global connectivity key to prosperity in an age of change
description: Rapid technological advances and the ever-changing geopolitical landscape and trade relations between nations are transforming economies around the world – while creating unprecedented opportunities and challenges for businesses and investors. These changes – and their implications for markets, public policies and long-term investment – were the focus of last month’s 29th edition of the Asian Investment Conference (AIC), which featured a week-long series of discussions and curated activities...

title: HSBC, Hang Seng mobile apps partly restored after glitches lock out Hong Kong customers
description: HSBC and its subsidiary Hang Seng Bank had resumed mobile phone service operations by lunchtime Monday after an earlier outage had locked customers out, but some online services were still temporarily unavailable, according to messages posted on the two lenders’ apps. HSBC, the biggest lender in the city with over 7 million customers in Hong Kong, had its mobile banking service interrupted, with many customers complaining they could not log on to the system on Monday morning. More than 10 HSBC...

title: Hong Kong, mainland China stocks surge as US-Iran deal drives crude to 3-month low
description: Hong Kong stocks kicked off the week’s trading on a strong note on Monday after the US said that it sealed a deal with Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and lift the US Navy blockade, sparking “risk-on” sentiment across asset classes. The Hang Seng Index rose 0.5 per cent to 24,840.37 as of 10.34am local time. The Hang Seng Tech Index gained 1.1 per cent. On the mainland, the CSI 300 Index climbed 1.5 per cent. Asian stocks also rallied. South Korea’s Kospi index, at the centre of the...

title: Oil Prices Plunge as U.S. and Iran Reach Deal to Reopen Strait of Hormuz
description: Oil prices plunged in early Monday trading in Asia after the U.S. and Iran announced a deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz more than 100 days after its closure. At the time of writing, Brent crude had dropped 3.95% to trade at $83.88 per barrel, while WTI had fallen 4.62% to $80.96 per barrel. Oil prices, which peaked in mid-May, have been slowly but surely trending downward in recent weeks on rumors of a deal, even after multiple escalatory strikes. On Sunday night, President Trump declared that a deal with Iran was complete, writing on social…

description: As an ideology, it’s over. But the relationship is not.

title: The End of Climate Politics
description: As the West debated a green energy future, Beijing was building it.

title: The End of the United Nations
description: To its most powerful member, it is worth more alive than dead.

title: The End of Asylum
description: States across the global north are shutting their doors permanently.

title: The End of Political Parties
description: They are hardly prepared for the new system.

title: The End of Chinese Growth
description: Beijing’s economic ambitions have been brought back to earth.

title: The End of Morality
description: We may come to miss the hypocrisy.

title: The End of the Future
description: Only Silicon Valley looks ahead now.

title: Sweden beat Tunisia 5-1 in strong start to World Cup
description: Two goals from Ayari and one each from Isak, Gyokeres and Svanberg take Sweden to the top of Group F in Monterrey.

title: Uruguay squad lands in US after flight delay ahead of World Cup opener
description: Uruguay arrived in the US less than 24 hours before the kickoff time for their game against Saudi Arabia.

title: Lebanese remain sceptical despite US-Iran ceasefire announcement
description: The US-Iran ceasefire announcement has raised hopes in Lebanon.

title: Ivory Coast beats Ecuador 1-0 at FIFA World Cup with Amad Diallo goal
description: Ecuador looked strong before Diallo broke through in the 90th minute with a clinical left-footed shot to seal the win.

description: Ecuadorian football fans packed Philadelphia’s Rocky Steps to cheer on the South American nation in its opening

title: US-Iran to sign a ‘peace deal’ on Friday: What we know so far
description: Trump says a US-Iran deal will be signed this Friday, as Tehran confirms plans to end hostilities.

title: Trump allies cheer Iran ‘peace deal’ announcement as Democrats seek clarity
description: US and Iran say memorandum of understanding to be signed on Friday, but no official terms yet released.

title: Zimbabwe’s climate migrants fear eviction as crackdown intensifies
description: Many settled in Zimbabwe’s fertile Eastern Highlands after drought left their home areas unable to support farming.

title: Iran’s Ghalenoei, Taremi decry US treatment before first World Cup game

title: Stock markets soar, oil falls as US, Iran confirm deal to end war
description: Asian stock markets surge as Washington and Tehran announce agreement to end hostilities and reopen Strait of Hormuz.

title: AI and global connectivity key to prosperity in an age of change
description: Rapid technological advances and the ever-changing geopolitical landscape and trade relations between nations are transforming economies around the world – while creating unprecedented opportunities and challenges for businesses and investors. These changes – and their implications for markets, public policies and long-term investment – were the focus of last month’s 29th edition of the Asian Investment Conference (AIC), which featured a week-long series of discussions and curated activities...

title: HSBC, Hang Seng mobile apps partly restored after glitches lock out Hong Kong customers
description: HSBC and its subsidiary Hang Seng Bank had resumed mobile phone service operations by lunchtime Monday after an earlier outage had locked customers out, but some online services were still temporarily unavailable, according to messages posted on the two lenders’ apps. HSBC, the biggest lender in the city with over 7 million customers in Hong Kong, had its mobile banking service interrupted, with many customers complaining they could not log on to the system on Monday morning. More than 10 HSBC...

title: Hong Kong, mainland China stocks surge as US-Iran deal drives crude to 3-month low
description: Hong Kong stocks kicked off the week’s trading on a strong note on Monday after the US said that it sealed a deal with Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and lift the US Navy blockade, sparking “risk-on” sentiment across asset classes. The Hang Seng Index rose 0.5 per cent to 24,840.37 as of 10.34am local time. The Hang Seng Tech Index gained 1.1 per cent. On the mainland, the CSI 300 Index climbed 1.5 per cent. Asian stocks also rallied. South Korea’s Kospi index, at the centre of the...

title: Oil Prices Plunge as U.S. and Iran Reach Deal to Reopen Strait of Hormuz
description: Oil prices plunged in early Monday trading in Asia after the U.S. and Iran announced a deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz more than 100 days after its closure. At the time of writing, Brent crude had dropped 3.95% to trade at $83.88 per barrel, while WTI had fallen 4.62% to $80.96 per barrel. Oil prices, which peaked in mid-May, have been slowly but surely trending downward in recent weeks on rumors of a deal, even after multiple escalatory strikes. On Sunday night, President Trump declared that a deal with Iran was complete, writing on social…

title: Orbio raises $21 million to automate hiring and onboarding for frontline workers
description: Orbio announces $21 Million Series A in round led by Dawn Capital.
